requirements.title: Mounted-store workflow gate
requirements.description: Add a CI gate that exercises installed/built CLI store creation or mounting, project binding, pending write, review/promote, recall, and no project-local knowledge fallback.

requirements.title: Release parity
requirements.description: Release workflow must run the same artifact-protecting gates as normal CI before publish.

requirements.title: Store-only invariant gates
requirements.description: Add invariant tests for source-of-truth, routing, provenance, privacy, and retired dual-root behavior.

constraints.constraint: Store-only readiness must be judged by invariant gates in addition to line/statement coverage.
constraints.rationale: Known gaps are fixture realism and omitted gates, not only coverage percentage.

constraints.constraint: CI must test the packaged CLI/bin entry and at least one mounted-store flow.
constraints.rationale: Direct dist invocation and minimal fixtures can miss user-path failures.

constraints.constraint: Release must run cold-start perf before publish.
constraints.rationale: Normal CI already has the p95 gate; release currently omits it.

constraints.constraint: New store-only behavior should begin with a named failing contract case or golden fixture delta.
constraints.rationale: Resolver red-suite practice exists but is not standardized across surfaces.

domain.problem_statement: Stress-test whether Fabric's store-only architecture needs upgraded testing and CI/CD strategy, and whether TDD-style write-red discipline is sufficiently thorough.

terminology.definition: Runtime knowledge source of truth is mounted stores under global Fabric home, not project-local .fabric/knowledge.
End-to-end user path using real mounted store state.
Failing-first contract suite or fixture ratchet.

non_goals.title: Promote experimental HTTP to main release gate
non_goals.rationale: HTTP remains quarantined unless explicitly restored.

insights.summary: The repo already has broad tests and a useful test wall; the missing layer is release-level mounted-store workflow assurance.
Release CI is weaker than normal CI because it omits perf and Windows smoke.
Write-red discipline exists in pockets but is not a uniform store-only contribution rule.

open_questions.question: Where should new red tests be standardized?
What mounted-store corpus sizes should perf fixtures use?
